safest_plan.entry_basis: zero_hub grade entry (4.0). NOTE: price has since fallen to ~3.30 on the chart (live 3.49), BELOW this level, so it is a pending reclaim trigger, not a live buy — wait for price to get back above 4.0 and hold.

safest_plan.stop_basis: No stop stated by any source. The chart's green support zone (~4.0) has ALREADY broken. Estimated stop below the recent swing low ~3.20 (estimated) — note that with price at 3.49 and entry at 4.0, the original setup's risk/reward is currently broken.

stance_by_source.summary: Graded long ladder: buy 4.0, scale out 4.6 / 5.4 / 6.3 / 7 / 10 — but only scored 6/10 with a 'partial' verdict.
Weekly chart shows price dropped to ~3.30, breaking down through the marked buy/support zone and falling sharply at the current candle.

conflicts: The grade says buy at 4.0, but the same-day weekly chart shows price has already fallen to ~3.30 (live 3.49) — below the entry — so the long setup never triggered cleanly and the buy zone's support has broken.
Even the bullish grade was only scored 6/10 with a 'partial' verdict, so the upside ladder was never high-conviction to begin with.
plain_english: Zero's plan was to buy this around 4.00 and ride it up toward 4.60, 5.40, 6.30, 7.00 and eventually 10 — and those upside targets do line up with real levels on the chart. The problem is the chart itself: as of the same date the price had already dropped to about 3.30 (now 3.49), falling through the buy zone instead of bouncing from it, and the plan was only rated a cautious 'partial' to start with. The buy never confirmed, so don't chase it here. Wait — only consider the long if price climbs back above 4.00 and holds; below the recent low near 3.20 the idea is dead for now.
